# Device Principal

<!-- Generated by go run ./tools/deviceprincipal -write-doc. DO NOT EDIT. -->

> Riido task: RIID-4868 [Contracts] device principal 소유권 SSOT 정의

Executable SSOT: `github.com/teamswyg/riido-contracts/deviceprincipal` plus `docs/20-domain/device-principal.riido.json`.

DevicePrincipal separates daemon credentials from browser user tokens and fixes account-owned device/runtime liveness evidence.

- Evidence artifact: `device-principal-evidence`
- API policy: `device_principal_enrollment.v1`; dependency fact: `device-runtime-liveness-stale-projection`
- Daemon refresh: runtime snapshot every `5` seconds; stale after `20` seconds

## Model

- Principals: `UserPrincipal`, `DevicePrincipal`
- Daemon headers: `X-Riido-Device-ID`, `X-Riido-Device-Secret`
- Client headers: `X-Riido-AI-Agent-Token`
- Ownership: `UserPrincipal -> Device`, `Device -> Runtime`, `Workspace -> Agent`, `Agent -> selected Runtime binding`
- Binding sources: `DevicePrincipal`, `latest daemon runtime snapshot`, `workspace-scoped agents with saved runtime_id`
- Hard exclusions: `team_id`, `teamId`, `OpenAPI task context URL`, `Open API key`, `X-Workspace-Api-Key`
- Secret non-exposure sinks: `browser local storage`, `cookies`, `webview JavaScript state`, `renderer IPC payloads`, `command-line arguments`, `daemon status output`, `logs, task evidence, SSE events, or generated client responses`

## Dependency Phrases

- daemon runtime snapshot every 5 seconds
- daemon process facts
- not been refreshed for 20 seconds
- stale runtimes must not be used for newly derived `AgentRuntimeBinding`

- AgentRuntimeBinding fields: `agent_id`, `daemon_id`, `device_id`, `runtime_id`, `runtime_provider`
- Invariant anchors: `daemon identity uses DevicePrincipal credentials, not browser JWT`, `workspace_id scopes agent APIs but does not own device records`, `device_secret is one-time readable and never exposed to generated frontend state`, `stale runtimes are historical records, not new assignment binding candidates`

## Evidence Loop

- Observe: DevicePrincipal ownership and secret-boundary rules existed as long prose plus policy strings, while CI could not prove the prose, API metadata, dependency map, and runtime binding DTO stayed aligned.
- Hypothesis: A small executable vocabulary package plus a verifier can turn the principal model into evidence instead of hand-maintained explanation.
- Execute: Verify package constants, generated API policy metadata, dependency-map liveness phrases, and assignment.AgentRuntimeBinding shape from one manifest.
- Evaluate: CI emits device-principal evidence JSON and regenerates this reader; drift in headers, cadence, policy rules, dependency phrases, or DTO fields fails the verifier.
- Retrospective: Future revocation, rotation, or storage changes should extend this manifest/tool before downstream repos reinterpret daemon identity semantics.
